canonical_value: In the figure, \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \), as defined below, are the phase-modulated and frequency-modulated waveforms, respectively, corresponding to the message signal \( m(t) \):
surface_forms: In the figure, \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \), as defined below, are the phase-modulated and frequency-modulated waveforms, respectively, corresponding to the message signal \( m(t) \):

normalized_value.text: In the figure, \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \), as defined below, are the phase-modulated and frequency-modulated waveforms, respectively, corresponding to the message signal \( m(t) \):

canonical_value: If the highest instantaneous frequencies of \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \) are the same, then the value of the ratio \( \frac{K_p}{K_f} \) is _____ seconds.
surface_forms: If the highest instantaneous frequencies of \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \) are the same, then the value of the ratio \( \frac{K_p}{K_f} \) is _____ seconds.

normalized_value.text: If the highest instantaneous frequencies of \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \) are the same, then the value of the ratio \( \frac{K_p}{K_f} \) is _____ seconds.

canonical_value: If the highest instantaneous frequencies of \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \) are the same, then the value of the ratio \( \frac{K_p}{K_f} \) is _____ seconds.
surface_forms: If the highest instantaneous frequencies of \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \) are the same, then the value of the ratio \( \frac{K_p}{K_f} \) is _____ seconds.

normalized_value.target_text: If the highest instantaneous frequencies of \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \) are the same, then the value of the ratio \( \frac{K_p}{K_f} \) is _____ seconds.

canonical_value:
In the figure, \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \), as defined below, are the phase-modulated and frequency-modulated waveforms, respectively, corresponding to the message signal \( m(t) \):

\[
S_{PM}(t) = \cos(1000\pi t + K_p m(t))
\]
and
\[
S_{FM}(t) = \cos\left(1000\pi t + K_f \int_{-\infty}^{t} m(\tau)d\tau\right)\right)
\]

where \( K_p \) is the phase deviation constant in radians/volt and \( K_f \) is the frequency deviation constant in radians/second/volt. If the highest instantaneous frequencies of \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \) are the same, then the value of the ratio \( \frac{K_p}{K_f} \) is _____ seconds.
surface_forms:
In the figure, \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \), as defined below, are the phase-modulated and frequency-modulated waveforms, respectively, corresponding to the message signal \( m(t) \):

\[
S_{PM}(t) = \cos(1000\pi t + K_p m(t))
\]
and
\[
S_{FM}(t) = \cos\left(1000\pi t + K_f \int_{-\infty}^{t} m(\tau)d\tau\right)\right)
\]

where \( K_p \) is the phase deviation constant in radians/volt and \( K_f \) is the frequency deviation constant in radians/second/volt. If the highest instantaneous frequencies of \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \) are the same, then the value of the ratio \( \frac{K_p}{K_f} \) is _____ seconds.

normalized_value.title: EEE-Bench 开放题
normalized_value.rewritten_question_text:
In the figure, \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \), as defined below, are the phase-modulated and frequency-modulated waveforms, respectively, corresponding to the message signal \( m(t) \):

\[
S_{PM}(t) = \cos(1000\pi t + K_p m(t))
\]
and
\[
S_{FM}(t) = \cos\left(1000\pi t + K_f \int_{-\infty}^{t} m(\tau)d\tau\right)\right)
\]

where \( K_p \) is the phase deviation constant in radians/volt and \( K_f \) is the frequency deviation constant in radians/second/volt. If the highest instantaneous frequencies of \( S_{PM}(t) \) and \( S_{FM}(t) \) are the same, then the value of the ratio \( \frac{K_p}{K_f} \) is _____ seconds.

canonical_value: As shown in the image, an ideal transformer has a primary-to-secondary coil turns ratio of 10:1. The primary coil is connected in series with an ideal ammeter and is powered by an AC voltage source \(u = 100\sqrt{2} \sin 100\pi t\ (\mathrm{V})\) with negligible internal resistance. The fixed resistors have values \(R_1 = R_2 = R_3 = 10\ \Omega\), and \(C\) is a capacitor. The ammeter reading is 0.28 A. What is the voltage across the capacitor? Answer with a floating-point number to one decimal place. ( ) V
